fifa_web.name: FIFA
fifa_web.domain: Official football portal and watch ecosystem
fifa_web.modality: Browse-first web product for football information, rankings, ticketing, and watch handoff
fifa_web.positioning: Browse-first football web ecosystem for official news, match-following, rankings, tournament discovery, ticketing trust, and FIFA+ watch handoff.

chesscom_web.name: Chess.com
chesscom_web.domain: Online chess play and learning platform
chesscom_web.modality: Action-first web product for play, review, analysis, and learning
chesscom_web.positioning: Action-first chess web platform for games, review, self-analysis, puzzles, lessons, study plans, tournaments, and fair-play-guided competitive play.

FIFA: Check scores, fixtures, and one key tournament story between classes

Follow official tournament news and rankings during office breaks

Start blitz games fast, use premoves, reduce distractions, keep momentum
External keyboard and mouse, noisy dorm room, many short sessions, time pressure
Review finished games, run self-analysis, and plan tournament play
Quiet desktop setup, longer study sessions, strong interest in accuracy and improvement

hci_findings: Match Centre, News, Rankings, Tickets & Hospitality
Navigation exposes key football information tasks.
Information scent; recognition over recall; efficient task entry
Lan can enter Match Centre from the top level instead of remembering a direct URL.
Story browsing supports exploratory scanning across FIFA topics.
Ethan narrows from broad news to a tournament story during a short office break.
Official/preferred ticket wording and resale/exchange note
Ticket media releases create a clear trust cue.
Maria avoids a third-party ticket tab because FIFA identifies FIFA.com/tickets as the source.
FIFA+ states the watch proposition and next action.
Maria can tell that the next step is to sign in or get started before watching.
FIFA+ supports both live football and time-shifted content.
Maria can choose a highlight if live content does not fit the family's schedule.
FIFA.com, inside.fifa.com, plus.fifa.com, Store, Collect, Rewards
Task paths span several sibling properties.
Context switching; mental model fragmentation; consistency
Lan expects a single football hub but is moved across different hosts for stories, tickets, and video.
The watch path changes brand context and account expectations.
Continuity break; trust friction; mode boundary
Maria pauses because the FIFA+ destination looks like a different service during family planning.
Dense rails increase scan cost before a user finds live or archive content.
Maria searches highlights but has to scan many rail items before choosing.
Ticket status is visible only as repeated-check guidance.
Visibility of system status; planning friction
Maria cannot tell when new family-trip availability may appear without repeatedly checking.
Story browsing is strong, but quick tasks require a second jump.
Lan reads a headline and then has to jump elsewhere for scores or tickets.
Play, Puzzles, Learn, Train, Watch, Community
Homepage navigation foregrounds core chess tasks.
Minh sees Learn and Puzzles without knowing internal feature names.
Random opponent, bot, friend, time controls, rated toggle, rating range
Game start is short and flexible.
Flexibility; user control; low entry friction
Alex starts a blitz game quickly or adjusts time control before matching.
Expanded board with only essential play controls
Focus Mode hides distractions while preserving clocks and resign/draw actions.
Attention support; reduced clutter; task focus
Alex makes decisions with a larger board and fewer side panels.
Graph, accuracy, classifications, coach, key moves, retry
Review turns a finished game into concrete feedback.
Feedback; reflection; error recovery; external cognition
Quynh sees a blunder classification, retries the move, and understands the alternative.
Move pieces, setup position, load FEN/PGN, history, collections
Analysis supports expert direct manipulation and artifact transfer.
Direct manipulation; expert control; transfer of artifacts
Quynh pastes a PGN, changes a line, and compares engine feedback.
Study plans reduce uncertainty about what to learn next.
Minh chooses a beginner plan after puzzles instead of guessing from all lessons.
Many primary areas and training paths
The broad surface can overwhelm first-time users.
Choice overload; weak progressive disclosure at entry
Minh sees Play, Learn, Train, Puzzles, and Watch before knowing the best beginner sequence.
Premoves are efficient but risky when the reply is unexpected.
Alex premoves a capture that remains legal but weak after a surprising reply.
Focus Mode can be hard to discover because related controls appear only near the board boundary.
Alex never enables Focus Mode because the icon appears only on hover.
Toggles, charts, lines, classifications, premium gates
Analysis surfaces many controls and access expectations at once.
Cognitive load; progressive disclosure gap; expectation mismatch
Minh opens analysis after a loss and cannot tell which chart or control matters first.
drawbacks: Ecosystem sprawl across sibling FIFA properties
Browse-first friction for quick utilitarian tasks
Menu and feature overload for novices
Focus Mode is hard to discover
solutions: Keep Match Centre, News, Rankings, Tickets, Watch at top level; move Store, Collect, and Rewards into More FIFA.
Slim header with one primary task row and one tucked ecosystem menu.
Recognition over recall; cognitive load reduction
Short task entry, office browsing, ticket planning
Fewer property jumps for common tasks.
Store and collectibles receive less top-level exposure.
Add five quick-intent chips under the hero: Scores, News, Rankings, Tickets, Watch.
Horizontal chip bar under page title.
Short sessions with unclear starting point
Users choose by intent before reading navigation labels.
One more row competes for above-fold attention.
Before leaving FIFA.com, show You are opening FIFA+ powered by DAZN with destination benefits and sign-in expectation.
Centered modal with Continue and Stay on FIFA.com.
Users understand why branding and login state change.
Adds one step for repeat viewers.
Add persistent Back to FIFA.com strip and tournament breadcrumb on FIFA+.
Thin top bar with FIFA icon, destination label, and return link.
FIFA+ watch browsing after ticket/news research
Users retain orientation across the watch boundary.
Provide filters for Live, Highlights, Documentaries, Competition, and Duration above rails.
Sticky filter row above content rails.
Users narrow a large content surface faster.
Filter accuracy depends on metadata quality.
Collapse nonmatching rails into section labels until expanded.
Condensed page with one expanded rail and folded sections.
Show Official sale, Resale open, Waiting room, Coming soon, and Latest update time by tournament.
Small status cards above ticket links.
Visibility of system status; planning support
Users know whether to act now or wait.
Requires reliable status data and governance.
Offer email and browser alerts by tournament and market, confirmed by receipt.
Alert drawer with checkbox list and next release note.
Family travel planning over many days
Add right rail or sticky side sheet for Scores today, Rankings latest, Tickets official source, Watch now.
Compact side module with four task cards.
Story readers can branch into utilitarian tasks in context.
Add context chips in article header: Open Match Centre, View Tickets, Watch on FIFA+.
Tournament article with adjacent action need
Users can act from the article without searching navigation again.
First-run chooser: Play now, Review last game, Learn basics, Solve puzzles, Join tournament.
Large task cards above default homepage feed.
New users see goals before feature taxonomy.
User pins top three tasks and hides unused modules for 30 days.
Compact home with pinned cards and Edit layout button.
May hide growth paths if poorly explained.
One-click preset keeps evaluation bar, best move, and plain-language coach notes.
Preset chips at top of analysis panel: Beginner, Standard, Expert.
Novices get a readable analysis surface.
Advanced controls become one step deeper.
Hover or tap chart and toggle labels for plain-language explanations.
Right-side info drawer with What this means text.
Analysis and Game Review study sessions
Users learn the meaning of graphs and labels in place.
Adds copy maintenance and localization work.
Mark each lesson, puzzle, and analysis feature with access labels before click.
Small pill tags: Free, 3/day, 1 lesson/day, Diamond.
Users know limits before investing attention.
Access labels may add visual noise.
After a limit wall, offer free alternative, reset timer, and queue for later.
Modal with Continue learning free, Save for later, Upgrade.
Study session after a daily limit
Users keep learning instead of stopping abruptly.
Show pending premoves with danger icon when opponent reply tree makes risk high.
Tiny queue strip above board, first item tinted warning color.
Users see what will execute before it happens.
One-click Clear or Esc clears queued premove chain.
Small Clear premoves pill next to clocks or settings.
Fast play after unexpected opponent move
Users recover from risky queued intent quickly.
After two full games, show Need fewer distractions? Try Focus Mode.
Post-game moment before next serious game
Users learn the hidden control when it is relevant.
Add Focus Mode toggle into board settings and keyboard shortcut help.
Settings panel item with live preview thumbnail.
Focus Mode becomes findable without hover discovery.

sources.title: Inside FIFA
claims_supported: Inside FIFA exposes Latest FIFA News and the global navigation labels Match Centre, News, Rankings, Tickets & Hospitality, Play, Inside FIFA, plus sibling destinations such as FIFA+, Store, Collect, and Rewards.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: All stories & topics
claims_supported: The all stories page supports exploratory browsing through categories, content types, articles, blogs, media releases, videos, and albums.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Blog
claims_supported: The FIFA World Cup 2026 blog functions as a tournament story hub with dated updates and story cards.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Ticketing Programme launches this September
claims_supported: FIFA directs fans to FIFA.com/tickets to register interest, create a FIFA ID, and follow phased ticket releases.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Last-Minute Sales Phase
claims_supported: FIFA.com/tickets is identified as the official and preferred ticket source; fans are asked to check it regularly; the official Resale/Exchange Marketplace is available for eligible ticket holders.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Watch FIFA+ Live Stream Online
claims_supported: FIFA+ presents a watch surface with sign-in or get-started controls, live or upcoming content, highlights, replays, documentaries, and archive content.
The FIFA+ destination is presented through a DAZN-branded page.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Match Centre
claims_supported: The Match Centre URL exposes fixtures, results, match details, competitions, and live-now filtering; some content is client-side rendered.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking
claims_supported: The rankings page exposes the latest men's ranking table, filters, official update dates, and ranking rules notes.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Tickets & Hospitality
claims_supported: The tickets URL is the official entry point for first-hand FIFA tournament ticket and hospitality information; the page is client-side rendered in crawled text.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Chess.com homepage
claims_supported: The homepage foregrounds Play, Puzzles, Learn, Train, Watch, Community, Get Started, lessons, bots, puzzles, and watching events.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do I start a game on Chess.com?
claims_supported: Users can start games from the homescreen or site-wide Play menu, using recent time control, custom settings, random opponent, bots, or friends.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: What are pre-moves and how do they work?
claims_supported: Premoves can be enabled and then entered while it is the opponent's turn; the feature saves time but executes automatically if legal.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: What is focus mode? How do I turn it on?
claims_supported: Focus Mode minimizes distractions by expanding the board and showing only the board, clocks, draw, and resign controls.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do I change my board size?
claims_supported: Board settings, Focus Mode, Theatre Mode, and Flip Board appear when hovering near the board/sidebar boundary, creating a discoverability issue for hidden controls.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How does Game Review work?
claims_supported: Game Review appears after a game and provides a detailed review flow with accuracy, move classifications, key moves, coach guidance, graphs, and retry learning.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do I use Game Analysis?
claims_supported: Game Analysis lets users revisit analyzed games and continue deeper study through analysis tools.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do I use the analysis board?
claims_supported: The Analysis Board supports direct manipulation, setup position, FEN/PGN loading, game history, collections, engine settings, evaluation bar, lines, arrows, and move feedback.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do Puzzles work on Chess.com?
claims_supported: Puzzles are reachable from the side menu or homepage and include puzzle of the day, rated puzzles, themes, Puzzle Rush, and Puzzle Battle.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: How do Lessons work on Chess.com?
claims_supported: Lessons are reached from Learn, use interactive practice challenges, and include access limits by membership level.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Chess Study Plans for All Levels
claims_supported: Study plans guide players by skill level and help organize training time through curated lessons and videos.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Chess.com tournaments schedule
claims_supported: Tournament schedules expose arena and prize tournament calendars such as Titled Tuesday, Arena Kings, Bullet Brawls, and variant events.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

sources.title: Cheating And Fair Play On Chess.com
claims_supported: The fair-play page explains competitive integrity, account closures, fair-play policy, event checks, and enforcement expectations.
sources.notes: Official English source. Vietnamese equivalent not surfaced during the source refresh.

assumptions: GROUP_ID and member names are placeholders.
Peer reviewer names are mock names for preparation only.
Official Vietnamese pages were not surfaced during the source refresh.
Mermaid source is preserved; PDF diagrams include readable text fallback.
